title: searched for products on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
source_description: User can add a product to the cart from the product detail page and see the cart item count update
expected_result:
The run attempted to use the site’s search box to find multiple products in sequence (first “iphone”, then “mac”, then “canon”).
It successfully entered “iphone” in the “Search For Products” field on the homepage and opened an iPhone product page (showing “iPhone” with an “Out Of Stock” label).
From the iPhone product page, it entered “mac” in the search field and clicked a “MacBook” result from the search suggestions, which navigated to a MacBook product page.
The run then tried to type “canon” into the search box while still on a product page, but the automation ended before it could complete the next action (for example, selecting a Canon result or loading a Canon product/search results page).
Why it likely failed: the flow appears incomplete—after typing “canon” there’s no recorded click on a search suggestion or the Search button, and no final page showing Canon results; the script may have stopped early or couldn’t find/activate the next expected search result element.
description: User can add a product to the cart from the product detail page and see the cart item count update
kane_objective: Navigate to product_id=28 — click Add to Cart — verify cart icon count updates. Stop once confirmed.

title: attempted to open the cart and select a product option on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
source_description: User can open the cart and see the list of added items with their names and prices
expected_result:
The run navigated to the LambdaTest eCommerce Playground site and opened the cart panel from the top-right cart icon.
The cart slide-out showed “Your shopping cart is empty!” with $0.00 totals, indicating nothing was successfully added to the cart at that point.
It then opened a product page (URL shows product_id=30). The visible product page in the screenshots is for “Canon EOS 5D” and shows a required option dropdown (“--- Please Select ---” with a red “Size required!” message), suggesting the product needs an option selected before it can be added to the cart.
A later step attempted to click a specific product option element (DOM id #input-option230-216836), likely to satisfy the required “Size” selection.
The automation failed immediately after that with “step file not found,” so it never completed the next action (such as adding the item to the cart or proceeding to checkout). This looks like a test/script issue rather than a user-visible site error, and it prevented finishing the shopping flow.
There is also a mismatch between the intended product (“Microsoft smartwatch” was clicked earlier) and the product page shown (“Canon EOS 5D”), which likely indicates the flow navigated to an unexpected product page or the product selection step did not land on the correct item.
description: User can open the cart dropdown and see the list of added items with their names and prices
kane_objective: Navigate to product_id=28 — add to cart — open cart dropdown — verify item name and price. Stop once confirmed.

title: opened the Laptops product catalog on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
source_description: User can navigate to the product catalog and see a list of products
expected_result:
Opened https://ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io/.
Used the site navigation to go to the product browsing/catalog area.
Landed on the Laptops category page where a grid of product items is visible.
description: User can navigate to the Laptops product catalog and see a grid of product listings
kane_objective: User can navigate to the Laptops product catalog and see a grid of product listings

title: attempted to filter Components by Apple on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
source_description: User can apply a brand filter from the sidebar to narrow product results
expected_result:
The run navigated to the LambdaTest Ecommerce Playground site and clicked “SHOP NOW.”
It opened the “Shop by Category” menu and selected the “Components” category, landing on the Components listing page (URL shows category path=25).
On the Components page, the left-side FILTER panel shows a “MANUFACTURER” section with “Apple” listed as an option, and the automation attempted to click the “Apple” manufacturer filter.
The run failed before confirming any filtered results (there’s no evidence of the product list updating after the Apple click). This commonly happens when the filter click doesn’t take effect—e.g., the page didn’t finish loading, the filter list was partially off-screen/collapsed, or the click landed on a non-clickable area of the manufacturer row.
Because the objective and final URL were not recorded in the run output, the last reliable state is the Components category page where the Apple filter was about to be applied.
description: User can apply a manufacturer brand filter from the sidebar to narrow product results
kane_objective: User can apply a manufacturer brand filter from the sidebar to narrow product results

title: opened a product detail page on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
source_description: User can click a product to open its detail page and see the name and price
expected_result:
Went to the LambdaTest Ecommerce Playground site.
Selected a product from the listing to open its details page.
Confirmed the product details page displayed the product name (HTC Touch HD) and price ($146.00).
description: User can click a product thumbnail to open its detail page and see the product name and price
kane_objective: User can click a product thumbnail to open its detail page and see the product name and price

title: browsed the homepage and product listings on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
source_description: User can browse products and the homepage without logging in
expected_result:
Opened the LambdaTest eCommerce Playground site as a guest user.
Browsed the homepage to confirm the main promotional banner/hero section was visible and the site could be viewed without logging in.
Finished on the homepage with browsing access confirmed.
description: User can browse the homepage and product listings without logging in as a guest
kane_objective: User can browse the homepage and product listings without logging in as a guest

title: searched for an iPhone on ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io
source_description: User can search for a product by name and see relevant results
expected_result:
Opened ecommerce-playground.lambdatest.io.
Used the site’s search bar to look up “iPhone”.
A results dropdown appeared under the search bar showing multiple “iPhone” product entries with prices, confirming the search worked.
description: User can search for a product by name using the search bar and see relevant results
kane_objective: User can search for a product by name using the search bar and see relevant results

description: User can register a new account by filling in first name last name email telephone and password fields
kane_objective: User can register a new account by filling in first name last name email telephone and password fields

description: User can log in with a registered email address and password and land on the account dashboard
kane_objective: Navigate to /account/login — enter credentials — click Login — verify dashboard URL. Stop immediately once confirmed.

description: User can log out from the account and be redirected to the home page
kane_objective: Log in — click My Account dropdown — click Logout — verify home page redirect. Stop once confirmed.

description: User can remove an item from the shopping cart and see the cart update with the item gone
kane_objective: Navigate to product_id=28 — add to cart — navigate to checkout/cart — click Remove — verify cart empty. Stop once confirmed.

description: User can update the quantity of an item in the shopping cart and see the line total recalculate
kane_objective: Navigate to product_id=28 — add to cart — navigate to checkout/cart — change qty to 2 — update — verify total recalculates. Stop once confirmed.

description: User can sort products by price low to high and see the listing order change
kane_objective: User can sort products by price low to high and see the listing order change

description: User can add a product to the wish list from the product detail page and view it in the wishlist
kane_objective: Log in — navigate to product_id=40 — click Add to Wish List — verify confirmation. Stop once wishlist confirmed.

description: User can complete a guest checkout by entering a shipping address and selecting flat rate shipping
kane_objective: Navigate to product_id=28 — add to cart — navigate to checkout — select Guest Checkout — fill address — select Flat Rate — verify proceed. Stop once shipping confirmed.
